name: Font del Nogueret - Font de Cabres Trail
name_original: Trail run circular a la cima del Pic de la Font de Cabres
description: Tackle the difficult 9.1-mile Font del Nogueret - Font de Cabres Trail for panoramic views and a challenging jog through varied terrain.
region_intro: Jogging routes around La Vall D'Uixó offer a diverse landscape, from the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park to the extensive citrus groves in the lower elevations. The region is characterized by rugged hills, lush cork oak forests, and cool ravines, providing varied running environments. Peaks reaching up to 700 meters, such as Pipa and Penyalba, offer challenging ascents and panoramic views. Additionally, historical aqueduct trails and routes near the Coves de Sant Josep add unique elements to the running experience. Best jogging routes around La Vall D'Uixó The most popular jogging route is Font del Nogueret - Font de Cabres Trail, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a substantial elevation gain through varied natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Bassa del Quistel loop from la Vall d'Uixó, a difficult 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route features significant climbs and descents, traversing the region's natural beauty. Local runners also love the Coll de L'Ereta loop from la Vall d'Uixó, a 13.6 miles (21.9 km) trail leading through the mountainous terrain surrounding La Vall D'Uixó, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Jogging around La Vall D'Uixó is defined by mountainous terrain, natural park trails, and historical aqueduct paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to challenging mountain runs. The routes in La Vall D'Uixó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from nearly 10 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore La Vall D'Uixó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around La Vall d'Uixó?
A: There are nearly 50 running routes available around La Vall d'Uixó, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels. This includes options from easier paths to challenging mountain runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: The running trails in La Vall d'Uixó offer diverse terrain. You'll find mountainous paths within the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Espadán Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by lush cork oak forests and rugged landscapes. Lower elevations feature extensive citrus groves, while historical aqueduct paths provide flatter, scenic options. Expect a mix of challenging ascents and descents, especially on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31494043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt del Pipa – Penya del Migdia loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, La Vall d'Uixó does offer some easier paths suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;strong&gt;Camí de l'Aigua (Water Route)&lt;/strong&gt; is an urban trail that follows an old irrigation channel, passing by historical sites and offering a gentler experience. You can also find paths along the San José and La Alcudia aqueducts which are generally less strenuous.

Q: What are the best times of year to go running in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: The best times of year for running in La Vall d'Uixó are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to run year-round, though some higher mountain trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running or walking options in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Yes, for families, the &lt;strong&gt;Senda XYZ (XYZ Line Trail)&lt;/strong&gt; offers an easy and historically interesting path, following a defense line from the Spanish Civil War. The &lt;strong&gt;Camí de l'Aigua&lt;/strong&gt;, an urban route tracing an old irrigation channel, is also suitable for a leisurely family jog or walk, passing by Roman aqueducts and medieval windmills.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Many of the natural trails and paths in the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park and surrounding areas are generally dog-friendly, provided your dog is well-behaved and kept under control, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Always ensure to check local regulations for specific trails, and remember to carry water for your pet, particularly on longer or more exposed routes.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while running in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: La Vall d'Uixó's routes are rich in landmarks. You can explore ancient Muslim towers and old agricultural structures on trails like the PR-V-164. The area is also home to the famous &lt;strong&gt;Coves de Sant Josep (San José Caves)&lt;/strong&gt;, known for Europe's longest navigable underground river. Nearby, you might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2880412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Vall d'Uixó&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ajuez Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4047351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Dehesa viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around La Vall d'Uixó are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Popular circular options include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14842297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa del Quistel loop&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31777763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll Roig (Red Pass) loop&lt;/a&gt;, which both offer significant climbs and descents through the region's natural beauty.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: The running routes in La Vall d'Uixó are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars. Runners frequently praise the diverse and challenging terrain, the stunning panoramic views from peaks like Pipa, and the opportunity to explore both natural park landscapes and historical sites. The variety of routes, from citrus groves to rugged mountains, is also a highlight.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while running?
A: Running through the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Espadán Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers excellent opportunities for wildlife spotting. This protected area is home to diverse flora and fauna, including various bird species, wild boars, and other small mammals. The lush cork oak forests and cool ravines provide a rich habitat, especially during quieter times of the day.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Parking availability varies by trail. For routes starting near urban areas or popular attractions like the Coves de Sant Josep, you'll typically find designated parking lots. For trails deeper within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, parking might be more limited, often consisting of roadside pull-offs or small clearings. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information before you go.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely! Many routes in La Vall d'Uixó provide spectacular panoramic views, especially those that ascend the surrounding mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31494043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt del Pipa – Penya del Migdia loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, includes an ascent to the peak of Pipa, offering extensive vistas of the area, including the Mediterranean Sea on clear days. The rugged terrain of the Sierra de Espadán also provides numerous viewpoints.
tips: From its 570 meters above sea level you will have guaranteed impressive panoramic views of the coast, the valleys of the Plana Baixa and in the distance those of the province of Valencia. Reaching the top is a very picturesque route and without too much technical difficulty, only at the end it will become a little more challenging but you will have the help of chains and ropes on the rock to assist you.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Font del Nogueret - Font de Cabres Trail for jogging?
faqs.answer: The trail is officially rated as difficult for jogging. While some sections feature comfortable tracks suitable for running, the ascent to Pic de la Font de Cabres becomes very technical and steep, with loose stones and sections that may require chains or ropes. Sustained jogging is difficult on these parts, making it more suitable for fast hiking or walking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter accessible paths, tracks, footpaths, and roads, with about 91% being car-free. The initial parts often feature comfortable tracks, but closer to the summit of Pic de la Font de Cabres, the path becomes more technical, with steep ascents, loose stones, and challenging sections that may require careful footing.

faqs.question: What kind of views and landmarks can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail offers impressive panoramic views, especially from the 570-meter summit of Pic de la Font de Cabres, where you can see the coast, the valleys of Plana Baixa, and even the distant province of Valencia. You'll also notice the distinctive 'red rodeno' earth and rocks. Along the way, you'll pass through oak woods and discover historical remnants like trenches and fortifications from the Spanish Civil War near the collado de Font de Cabres.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is a circular route starting from Vall d'Uixó, it's best to look for parking options within or on the outskirts of Vall d'Uixó. Specific parking areas for the PR-CV 164 route are usually well-marked in the town.

faqs.question: Is the Font del Nogueret - Font de Cabres Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the technical and steep sections, especially near Pic de la Font de Cabres, ensure your dog is agile and accustomed to challenging terrain. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially in the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, to protect wildlife and for their safety.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit. The temperatures are milder, making the strenuous sections more manageable. Summers can be very hot, and while winters are usually mild, the higher, more exposed sections can be windy and cooler. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike or jog the Font del Nogueret - Font de Cabres Trail. It is located within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, which is freely accessible to the public. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the main route is the Font del Nogueret - Font de Cabres trail, it does intersect with sections of other paths. You'll find parts of the Camí de l'Anoueret, Senda del Frontó, Senda Caçadors, Camí del Canyaret, Senda Racó de Granell, and Senda Racó de la Bessona along the way.

faqs.question: Are there any water sources or amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll find natural water sources like Font del Nogueret and Font de Cabres, which can be refreshing stops. However, it's always advisable to carry sufficient water, especially on warmer days, as the reliability of natural fountains can vary. For cafes or other amenities, you would need to visit Vall d'Uixó before or after your trip.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this trail, especially for the challenging sections?
faqs.answer: For the challenging sections, especially near Pic de la Font de Cabres, sturdy hiking shoes with good grip are essential due to loose stones and steep inclines. Consider bringing gloves if you anticipate using chains or ropes. Layered clothing is recommended, as conditions can change. Always carry enough water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and sun protection.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While some initial parts are accessible, the overall trail is rated as difficult due to significant elevation changes and technical sections, particularly the ascent to Pic de la Font de Cabres. It requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness. Beginners might find it very challenging, especially if attempting to jog the entire route. It's more suitable for experienced hikers or trail runners.

waypoints.name: Pic de la Font de Cabres
